title: BLOG
author: Admin
paragraphs: In the final week of May, the Live4Help Foundation moved beyond plans and proposals, bringing its mission to life through meaningful, on-ground engagement. By connecting directly with donors, CSR leaders, institutional partners, and grassroots volunteers, the Foundation reaffirmed a powerful belief: True impact is built on trust, relationships, and human connection.
From Conversations to Commitments
What began as dialogue quickly evolved into decisive action. These interactions were not routine meetings; they became catalysts for deeper partnerships and long-term impact.
Each conversation reinforced a shared vision—to move beyond intent and deliver measurable, meaningful outcomes.
Driving Change Where It Matters Most

At the heart of Live4Help’s approach lies a deep commitment to working closely with underserved communities. Through direct engagement, the Foundation ensures that every initiative is guided by real needs and designed for lasting transformation.
Key focus areas include:
These efforts are not standalone interventions; they are part of an integrated strategy to build self-reliant, resilient communities.
Proof That Change is Possible
Behind every initiative lies a story of transformation.

Stories like that of Anjali Kisku—who rose from adversity to independence through sustained support—serve as powerful reminders of what is possible when compassion meets commitment. These are not just individual successes; they represent the ripple effect of collective action and sustained investment in people.
An Invitation to Create Impact Together

As we continue this journey, the opportunity ahead is significant. Today, we invite you to be part of this movement. Your generous support will help us scale these proven interventions, reach more vulnerable communities, needy and bright students, and build long-term, sustainable ecosystems of change
Together, we can transform lives—not just for today, but for generations to come. Your partnership can help transform lives—not just today, but for generations to come.
Together, we can turn purpose into enduring impact.

Restoring Coastal Resilience Through CSR: Mangrove Plantation Initiative in Sundarban, West Bengal
🎓 A Defining Milestone in Our Journey: Supporting Over 100 Students Through Education

title: BLOG
author: Admin
paragraphs: Live 4 Help Foundation (L4H) advanced its environmental sustainability mission through a large-scale mangrove restoration initiative in the Sundarban region of West Bengal. Under this initiative, 5,000 native mangrove saplings were planted on May 30th, 2026, to help strengthen fragile coastal ecosystems, enhance biodiversity, and build long-term climate resilience in one of India’s most vulnerable ecological zones.

The plantation included indigenous mangrove species such as Sundari, Bain, Kakra, Garjan, Golpata, and Dhuldhul, carefully selected for their adaptability and critical role in sustaining the biodiversity and stability of coastal mangrove habitats.

Mangrove ecosystems play a critical role in coastal protection, carbon sequestration, biodiversity preservation, and sustaining the livelihoods of communities living in climate-sensitive regions. This initiative reflects a practical, nature-based solution to environmental restoration while contributing to long-term ecological balance, disaster risk reduction, and community resilience.

This project was generously supported by M/s ABS Professional India Pvt Ltd under its Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) programme, demonstrating a meaningful commitment to environmental stewardship and sustainable community impact. Live 4 Help Foundation sincerely acknowledges Mr. Piyush Jagadish for his leadership in enabling this CSR collaboration and Mr. Kaushik Bhattacharya for his valued encouragement and support.

L4H also recognizes the dedicated contribution of Dr. Animesh Mandal and his team (Asim Das, Mihir Mistry, Sabita Sardar, Jharna Mandal, Bijali Das, and Nandita Sardar), whose on-ground coordination, community engagement, and implementation support were instrumental to the successful execution of the plantation drive. Special thanks to Animesh and his family to host a lovely luncheon during the event.

The Foundation was encouraged by the strong participation of its members and well-wishers, who not only joined the plantation activity but also reviewed and assessed the impact of earlier plantation drives conducted over the years. It was deeply inspiring to witness stretches of riverbanks now turning green due to the steady growth of mangroves planted in previous phases, including sites restored just months earlier. At the same time, visible soil erosion in adjoining areas served as a powerful reminder of the urgency and importance of sustained restoration efforts. The heartfelt words of appreciation from local resident Razia Sultana further reaffirmed the value of this initiative for the community. The Foundation extends sincere thanks to Kajal Mandal, Debashis Das, and Tarun Maiti for their coordination and preparation of the event, and acknowledges the presence and participation of Joyita Maiti, Ajay Ray, Ambarish Das, and Bibekananda Maiti.

The Foundation remains committed to post-plantation care through regular monitoring, replacement of damaged saplings, and repair of protective fencing affected by river currents and extreme weather. This sustained maintenance approach is essential to ensuring healthy establishment, long-term survival, and measurable environmental impact.

As part of its ongoing environmental mission, Live 4 Help Foundation welcomes partnerships with corporate organisations to support future phases of its mangrove restoration programme through CSR sponsorships. Such collaborations can help expand plantation coverage, strengthen long-term maintenance, and generate lasting ecological and community impact across vulnerable coastal regions.
Please find attached a few photographs from the activity for your perusal. These images capture the spirit of the initiative and the collective efforts of everyone involved.

title: BLOG
author: Admin
paragraphs: At Live 4 Help Foundation, education has always been more than a program – it has been a promise. A promise to stand beside children and young adults whose aspirations are strong, but whose access to opportunity is limited by circumstance.

In FY 2025–26, we reached a moment that will forever remain special in our journey: for the first time since inception, the cumulative number of students supported through our education initiative crossed 100.
This milestone is not just a number. It represents 100+ individual stories of resilience, hope, and determination – and countless families who believe that education can transform lives.
🌱 Growing Step by Step: From Vision to Impact
What began as a small effort to support a handful of deserving students has today grown into a multi‑state education support program spanning 11 states and 47 institutions.
As of April 2026:
This steady growth reflects not scale for the sake of numbers, but thoughtful, need‑based support, renewed year after year.
📍 Education That Reaches Where It’s Needed Most
Our students come from diverse regions across India, with major representation from:
Each dot on the map represents students by state with a dream and inner urge, drive and a quiet determination to change their future.
💙 Beyond Fees: What Support Truly Means
Our educational assistance goes far beyond tuition support. It includes:
Over the years, education spend has grown steadily, reaching its highest level in FY 2025–26, demonstrating our long‑term commitment, not one‑time charity.
🌟 This Milestone Belongs to Our Supporters
Crossing the 100‑student mark would not have been possible without:
Every contribution – big or small – has helped convert aspiration into action.
🤝 Join Us in Shaping the Next 100 Stories
While we celebrate this milestone, our mission continues. There are many more children standing on the edge of opportunity, waiting for someone to believe in them.
We invite:
📩 Write to us | Partner with us | Sponsor an education journey
Together, we can ensure that the next milestone comes not just faster—but stronger, deeper, and wider.
Because when education changes one’s life, it uplifts a whole generation.

title: BLOG
author: Admin
paragraphs: Cancer awareness is not only medical information—it is the work of replacing fear and stigma with knowledge, empathy, and timely action. Live4Help Foundation is committed to helping communities recognise early warning signs and seek screening without hesitation.

Programme snapshot (24 March 2026): Live4Help Foundation conducted a Cancer Awareness Programme with a primary focus on breast cancer awareness, emphasising early detection and preventive screening. The session strengthened confidence around seeking care as an act of strength and self-care.
Sponsored by: M/S. Erbe Medical India Pvt Ltd. Support: Mr. Prasun Dutta.

With the support of Darjeeling Hills University: Registrar Dr. Sujata Rani Rai, Mr. Debraj Ghotak, Mr. Jitendra Singh and Mr. Lalit Rajbonshi. Their guidance helped ensure smooth implementation. The programme reached 150 students and family members, extending awareness to both youth and caregivers.

Community partner: Roymoni Smriti Foundation, led by Prof. Dr. Animesh Mandal, with Mr. Madhab Sardar, Mr. Koushik Biswas, Mr. Manoj Mondal and Dr. Manorajan Mandal, for beneficiary coordination and community engagement.

The attached snaps and clippings reflect the programme’s reach and reinforce our belief that awareness inspires action and saves lives. Live4Help Foundation aims to expand these sessions across underserved communities, strengthen early-detection efforts, and support sustained follow-up care.

Call for collaboration: We invite corporate organisations, institutions and CSR partners to support awareness, early detection, and community empowerment—so no one suffers in silence and timely care reaches those who need it most.

title: BLOG
author: Admin
paragraphs: On 21 March 2026, Live4Help Foundation organised a community medical camp at Ashapur Tea Garden, Naxalbari, Darjeeling (West Bengal), to support daily-wage workers, agricultural labourers, and economically disadvantaged residents from nearby villages who have limited access to regular healthcare. The initiative was generously sponsored by M/s ERBE Medical India Private Ltd., New Delhi, India.

The camp benefited a total of 205 people (out of which 105 gave blood samples) and was conducted under the supervision of Dr. Manorajan Mandal and Dr. Pinki Goyala, with on-ground support from trained healthcare staff from Thyrocare. Participants received medical consultations and health guidance to enable early identification of potential concerns. Medical reports were distributed on March 25th, and follow-up consultations were recommended wherever required to help ensure continuity of care and reinforce the Foundation’s commitment to preventive healthcare.

The Foundation extends its heartfelt gratitude to volunteers from Roymoni Smriti Foundations—Mr Kaushik Biswas, Mr Madhab Sardar, and Mr Biswarup Das—and especially to Dr. Animesh Mandal for his leadership and effective coordination with all stakeholders, which played a key role in the success of the initiative. The Foundation would also like to thank DPSC Chairman Dr. Dilip Kumar Roy, Mr. Prahalad Roy, Afasana Khatun, Mr. Dhananjay Mahato, Putul Roy, and the rest of the Ashapur Tea Estate team for their dedicated support.
Through such initiatives, Live4Help Foundation continues to advance its mission of improving access to quality healthcare and strengthening community well-being.

Live4Help Foundation is committed to expanding access to primary healthcare for underserved communities. Sponsorship support enables us to organize additional medical camps, subsidize diagnostic tests, provide essential medicines, and strengthen last-mile community outreach.

To extend the reach of future medical camps, Live4Help Foundation invites sponsorship from corporates, institutions, and individuals who share a commitment to community welfare. Contributions will directly support consultations, medicines, diagnostics, and follow-up care for families with limited access to healthcare.
